segments.text: Imitate me, just as I also imitate Christ.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I praise you, brethren, that you remember me in all things and keep the traditions just as I delivered them to you.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I want you to know that the head of every man is Christ, the head of woman is man, and the head of Christ is God.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Every man praying or prophesying, having his head covered, dishonors his head.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered dishonors her head, for that is one and the same as if her head were shaved.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: If a woman is not covered, let her also be shorn. But if it is shameful for a woman to be shorn or shaved, let her be covered.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: A man indeed ought not to cover his head, since he is the image and glory of God; but woman is the glory of man.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Man is not from woman, but woman from man.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Nor was man created for the woman, but woman for the man.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For this reason the woman ought to have a symbol of authority on her head, because of the angels.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Nevertheless, neither is man independent of woman, nor woman independent of man, in the Lord.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For as woman came from man, even so man also comes through woman; but all things are from God.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Judge among yourselves. Is it proper for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Does not even nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a dishonor to him?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But if a woman has long hair, it is a glory to her; for her hair is given to her for a covering.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But if anyone seems to be contentious, we have no such custom, nor do the churches of God.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: In giving these instructions I do not praise you, since you come together not for the better but for the worse.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For first of all, when you come together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you, and in part I believe it.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For there must also be factions among you, that those who are approved may be recognized among you.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Therefore when you come together in one place, it is not to eat the Lord's Supper.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For in eating, each one takes his own supper ahead of others; and one is hungry and another is drunk.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: What! Do you not have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you despise the church of God and shame those who have nothing? What shall I say to you? Shall I praise you in this? I do not praise you.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I received from the Lord that which I also delivered to you:
that the Lord Jesus on the same night in which He was betrayed took bread;
When He had given thanks, He broke it and said,
Take, eat; this is My body which is broken for you; do this in remembrance of Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: In the same manner He also took the cup after supper, saying,
This cup is the new covenant in My blood. This do, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For as often as you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord's death till He comes.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Therefore whoever eats this bread or drinks this cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be guilty of the body and blood of the Lord.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But let a man examine himself, and so let him eat of the bread and drink of the cup.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For he who eats and drinks in an unworthy manner eats and drinks judgment to himself, not discerning the Lord's body.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For this reason many are weak and sick among you, and many sleep.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For if we would judge ourselves, we would not be judged.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But when we are judged, we are chastened by the Lord, that we may not be condemned with the world.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Therefore, my brethren, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But if anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, lest you come together for judgment. And the rest I will set in order when I come.
segments.speaker: Paul
